_comment: Editorial backlog. Picker reads sequentially; oldest-unpublished wins. Every topic is pre-mapped to a doctor from research/doctor_registry.md. LLM only writes prose — never picks doctors or sources.

topics.mechanism_brief: Acts as a pro-hormone (calcitriol) binding nuclear VDR; regulates 200+ genes incl. calcium absorption, immune modulation (Th1/Th2 balance), insulin sensitivity. Indian skin pigmentation + indoor lifestyle = ~70-90% deficiency rates in urban India per ICMR.
topics.anchor_seed: Vitamin D isn't a vitamin — it's a hormone your skin makes from sunlight, and most urban Indians are quietly running on empty.

topics.mechanism_brief: EPA + DHA incorporate into cell membranes, displacing arachidonic acid. Lower triglycerides via reduced hepatic VLDL secretion. Resolvins/protectins downstream reduce vascular inflammation. Indian vegetarian diets miss EPA/DHA almost entirely (ALA conversion <5%).
topics.anchor_seed: Your cell membranes are built from the fats you eat — most Indians are building theirs from inflammation-prone seed oils, not the omega-3s their cells actually want.

topics.mechanism_brief: Cofactor for methionine synthase (methylation, DNA synthesis) and methylmalonyl-CoA mutase (myelin maintenance). Found exclusively in animal foods. Vegetarian Indians + metformin users + PPIs all deplete it. Deficiency masquerades as fatigue, brain fog, neuropathy.
topics.anchor_seed: Your nerves and your DNA both need B12 — and if you don't eat animal foods, your body has no plan B.

topics.mechanism_brief: Required for hemoglobin (O2 transport), myoglobin, cytochromes (cellular ATP). Indian women: ~57% anemic per NFHS-5. Bisglycinate form bypasses the gut iron-block (hepcidin), tolerated 4× better than ferrous sulfate, no constipation.
topics.anchor_seed: Half of Indian women are running on low-octane blood — and the cheap iron tablets they're prescribed are the reason most of them quit.

topics.mechanism_brief: Phosphocreatine system regenerates ATP in <2s; the body's express-lane energy buffer. Most-studied supplement in sports science (>1000 RCTs). Cognitive benefit shown in vegetarians (lower baseline). Single proven dose: 3-5g/day, no loading needed.
topics.anchor_seed: Creatine isn't just for lifters — it's the cellular battery your brain runs on too, and vegetarians start the day three-bars low.

topics.mechanism_brief: Cofactor in 300+ enzymes incl. carbonic anhydrase, alcohol dehydrogenase, immune cell zinc-finger TFs. Critical for taste, smell, wound healing, testosterone synthesis. Indian phytate-heavy diets (cereals, legumes) bind zinc, dropping bioavailability by 50%.
topics.anchor_seed: Zinc runs your immune system, your sense of taste, and your hormones — and the same Indian foods that keep you full are quietly stealing it.

doctor.name: Dr. Issac Mathai
doctor.institution: Soukya International Holistic Health Centre, Bangalore

topics.mechanism_brief: Withanolides modulate HPA axis — lower morning cortisol by 14-23% in stressed adults (RCTs). Improves DHEA-S, reduces perceived anxiety. Ayurvedic 'rasayana' with strongest modern evidence. KSM-66 = standardized extract, root-only, 5% withanolides.
topics.anchor_seed: Ashwagandha doesn't sedate you — it lowers the cortisol you didn't realize you'd been carrying since 7am.

topics.mechanism_brief: Activates matrix Gla protein (MGP) which traps calcium in bone, away from arteries. Without K2, supplemented calcium can deposit in coronary plaques. MK-7 form has 72-hour half-life vs MK-4's 1-hour. Found in natto, hard cheeses, egg yolk.
topics.anchor_seed: Vitamin K2 is the traffic cop that tells calcium to go to your bones, not your arteries — and most calcium supplements forget to invite it.

topics.mechanism_brief: Cofactor for glutathione peroxidase (antioxidant), iodothyronine deiodinases (T4→T3 conversion). Brazil nuts contain 50-90mcg each — 1-2 covers daily need. Indian soils are selenium-poor; thyroid-disorder prevalence is rising in parallel.
topics.anchor_seed: Selenium is what lets your thyroid actually use the iodine you eat — and Indian soil ran out of it generations ago.

topics.mechanism_brief: Soluble viscous fiber forms gel in gut: slows gastric emptying (glucose curve flattens), binds bile acids (LDL drops as liver pulls cholesterol to make new bile), feeds butyrate-producing colonic bacteria. 7g/day cuts post-meal glucose 20-25% in T2D.
topics.anchor_seed: Psyllium is the cheapest, most-studied way to flatten a glucose spike — and it costs less than your morning chai.

topics.mechanism_brief: L-threonate carrier crosses blood-brain barrier where other Mg forms can't. MIT study (2010) showed 15% rise in brain Mg, with measurable working-memory + spatial learning improvements in older adults. Niche use: cognitive aging, not bulk Mg correction.
topics.anchor_seed: If magnesium glycinate is the volume knob for your nerves, threonate is the only form that gets past the velvet rope into your brain.

doctor.name: Dr. Bharat Aggarwal
doctor.institution: MD Anderson Cancer Center (formerly), now Inflammation Research

topics.mechanism_brief: Curcumin inhibits NF-κB → broad anti-inflammatory cascade. Bioavailability is the catch: <1% absorbed alone. Piperine (black pepper, 20mg) raises absorption 2000% by inhibiting glucuronidation in gut wall. Liposomal/Meriva forms further help.
topics.anchor_seed: Turmeric without pepper is a vitamin pill you swallow whole and excrete unopened — your grandmother knew this; your supplement aisle forgot.

topics.mechanism_brief: Precursor to acetylcholine (memory, attention) and phosphatidylcholine (cell membranes). Egg yolks + liver are top sources — vegetarians often deficient. Alpha-GPC crosses BBB, raises plasma choline 86%. NIH adequate intake: 425-550mg, most adults eat <300.
topics.anchor_seed: Acetylcholine is the chemistry of focus — and most vegetarians are trying to focus while running their brain on a fuel shortage.

topics.mechanism_brief: Most-studied single probiotic strain (>1000 RCTs). Reduces antibiotic-associated diarrhea by 50%, shortens infectious diarrhea in kids. Strain-specific evidence — generic 'probiotic' yogurt likely doesn't contain it. Look for ATCC 53103 strain ID on label.
topics.anchor_seed: Most probiotics on shelves don't list the strain — and strain matters more than the species, the brand, or the price.

topics.mechanism_brief: Cofactor for collagen hydroxylation (skin, vessels, joints), iron absorption (reduces Fe³⁺→Fe²⁺ in gut), neurotransmitter synthesis (dopamine→norepinephrine). Cooking destroys it. Shorter cold duration evidence is real but modest (~8-14% in adults).
topics.anchor_seed: Vitamin C doesn't prevent colds — but it does run the chemistry that builds your skin, your vessels, and the iron-loading port in your gut.

topics.mechanism_brief: Mitochondrial electron transport chain (Complex I→III). Endogenous synthesis drops 50% by age 60. Statins block the same HMG-CoA pathway → CoQ10 falls 30-50%, contributing to muscle pain. Ubiquinol (reduced form) absorbed 4× better than ubiquinone.
topics.anchor_seed: Statins lower your cholesterol and your CoQ10 in the same step — your muscles need both, and only one of them gets replaced.

topics.mechanism_brief: Required for T4 and T3 synthesis (each thyroxine molecule has 4 iodine atoms). Iodized salt program lifted India's deficiency rate from 50% to <30% but pink/Himalayan salt isn't iodized — quietly reversing gains in urban kitchens.
topics.anchor_seed: The fancy pink salt that replaced your iodized salt is also undoing fifty years of Indian public-health work in your thyroid.

topics.mechanism_brief: Precursor to glutathione (master antioxidant). Mucolytic (breaks disulfide bonds in mucus). ER-standard antidote for paracetamol overdose. Emerging evidence in PCOS insulin sensitivity, COPD exacerbations. Cheap, decades of safety data.
topics.anchor_seed: NAC is the only over-the-counter molecule that hospitals also keep in their crash carts — and most people have never heard of it.

topics.mechanism_brief: Trace mineral. Slows magnesium and calcium urinary loss. Raises free testosterone (~30% in small trials) by lowering SHBG. May modulate estrogen metabolism. RDA undefined but 3-10mg/day shows benefit. Found in raisins, prunes, almonds.
topics.anchor_seed: Boron is the trace mineral nobody talks about — and removing it from a diet quietly drops free testosterone in a way no one tracks.

topics.mechanism_brief: Sole carotenoids deposited in macula; filter blue light, quench free radicals from photoreceptor metabolism. AREDS2 trial: 10mg lutein + 2mg zeaxanthin slowed AMD progression. Egg yolk + leafy greens are top sources. Screen-heavy adults under-deliver.
topics.anchor_seed: Lutein and zeaxanthin are sunglasses your eyes build from the food you eat — and screen-heavy diets keep arriving without them.

topics.mechanism_brief: Activates AMPK (the same target metformin hits). Meta-analysis: 1500mg/day lowers HbA1c ~0.7%, comparable to metformin in mild T2D. Lowers LDL ~25mg/dL via PCSK9 modulation. Bioavailability is the catch: ~5%. Berberine + milk thistle improves it.
topics.anchor_seed: Berberine hits the same enzyme as metformin — but most people who take it don't realize they're using the wrong dose to feel anything.

topics.mechanism_brief: Cofactor for 5 carboxylase enzymes (gluconeogenesis, fatty acid synthesis, amino acid catabolism). True deficiency rare — gut bacteria synthesize it. Hair/nail benefit evidence is weak (most studies in actual deficiency). Causes false thyroid + troponin lab results above 5mg.
topics.anchor_seed: Biotin is the most over-sold vitamin in the supplement aisle — and it's probably distorting your blood tests at the dose on the label.

doctor.name: Dr. Issac Mathai
doctor.institution: Soukya International Holistic Health Centre, Bangalore

topics.mechanism_brief: Salidroside + rosavins modulate HPA axis acutely (within hours, unlike ashwagandha's weeks). Improved mental fatigue scores in night-shift physicians (Darbinyan 2007). Standardized: 3% rosavins, 1% salidroside. Take in morning — can disrupt sleep if late.
topics.anchor_seed: Rhodiola works the way coffee wishes it could — same alertness, no jitter, no crash, and your cortisol stays inside its lane.

topics.mechanism_brief: Lipid-phase antioxidant, protects PUFAs in cell membranes from oxidation. The 'vitamin E' on most labels is alpha-tocopherol alone — but gamma-tocopherol does the actual reactive-nitrogen-species mopping. Mixed tocopherols match what food delivers.
topics.anchor_seed: Most vitamin E pills give you one of the eight molecules nature ships together — and the one they pick isn't the one that does the heavy lifting.

topics.mechanism_brief: Inhibitory neurotransmitter (with GABA). 3g pre-bed lowers core body temp (faster sleep onset), improves subjective sleep quality + next-day fatigue scores. Precursor to collagen + glutathione. Sweet taste — easy to take in water. Cheap.
topics.anchor_seed: Glycine drops your body temperature the way your body wants to drop it before sleep — three grams in water, fifteen minutes earlier than usual.

topics.mechanism_brief: Cyanobacterium, 60-70% protein by dry weight, complete amino profile. Contains phycocyanin (anti-inflammatory pigment), B12 analogues (note: not bioavailable B12 for vegans). Heavy-metal contamination is the real risk — source-quality matters more than dose.
topics.anchor_seed: Spirulina is sold as a vegan B12 fix and isn't — but the real reason it earns its place is somewhere else entirely.

topics.mechanism_brief: Sulfur-containing AA, abundant in heart + retina + WBCs. 2023 Yamanaka-team paper (Singh et al., Science): plasma taurine drops with age in mice/monkeys/humans; supplementation restored markers in animals. Human trials ongoing. Vegan diets miss it.
topics.anchor_seed: Taurine is the amino acid your body manufactures less of every year — and the 2023 Yamanaka paper put it on the longevity-research map for a reason.

topics.mechanism_brief: Cofactor for pyruvate dehydrogenase (entry to Krebs cycle). Both water- and fat-soluble — the 'universal antioxidant'. Strongest evidence in diabetic peripheral neuropathy: 600mg IV (German ALADIN trials) reduced symptom scores meaningfully.
topics.anchor_seed: Alpha-lipoic acid is one of the few antioxidants that gets prescribed in German hospitals for nerve damage — and you can buy it on Amazon India.

topics.mechanism_brief: Cofactor for 4 enzymes incl. xanthine oxidase (purine→uric acid), sulfite oxidase (sulfite→sulfate detox). True deficiency essentially nonexistent in mixed diets — but underlies sulfite intolerance (wine headaches, dried fruit reactions).
topics.anchor_seed: Molybdenum is the trace mineral most people will never need to think about — except the ones whose wine headaches no doctor has explained.

doctor.name: Dr. Issac Mathai
doctor.institution: Soukya International Holistic Health Centre, Bangalore

topics.mechanism_brief: Crocin + safranal modulate serotonin and dopamine reuptake. Multiple Iranian RCTs: 30mg/day matched fluoxetine 20mg in mild-to-moderate depression at 6 weeks. Notable absence of SSRI side effects. Cost is the catch — saffron is expensive by gram.
topics.anchor_seed: Saffron is the costliest spice in your kitchen — and at 30mg/day it matched fluoxetine in head-to-head trials, with none of the SSRI baggage.
